articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Mexico fights back to 2-2 draw with Socceroos
article.description: A second-half equalizer from debutant Cesar Huerta earned a 2-2 draw for Mexico against Australia on Saturday in Dallas, Texas.
article.linkText: Mexico fights back to 2-2 draw with Socceroos
article.story:
A second-half equalizer from debutant Cesar Huerta earned a 2-2 draw for Mexico against Australia on Saturday in Dallas, Texas.

Australia took the lead in the friendly match through a goal from defender Harry Souttar in the 16th minute. Following a cross from Martin Boyle off a corner kick, Souttar headed the ball past opposing goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa.

Despite lacking a strong majority of possession, the Socceroos did well to defend in the first 45 minutes and had few issues with maintaining their 1-0 advantage by the half-time whistle.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga & more (U.S.)

Following the break, Mexico were then given a lifeline through a penalty after Keanu Baccus deflected a shot from El Tri's Luis Romo with his hand in the 18-yard box. With a chance to equalize, forward Santiago Giménez stepped up but failed to score, firing his shot into the woodwork.

Moments later, Australia were given a penalty of their own after Riley McGree was fouled by Romo in Mexico's box. Boyle slotted the ball into the net during the 63rd minute, raising the scoreline to 2-0 in the Socceroos' favor.

Mexico then gained another opportunity from the spot after Australia's Cameron Burgess -- another debutant -- knocked over Uriel Antuna in the box. Recently stepping onto the pitch as a substitute, Fulham forward Raúl Jiménez made no mistake with his spot-kick, bringing Australia's lead down to 2-1.

Energized by additional tactical changes made by manager Jaime "Jimmy" Lozano, Mexico continued to increase their attacking pressure, eventually finding the net once again in the 83rd minute off a goal from substitute Huerta in his first match with the national team.

Galvanized by the goal, El Tri pressured Australia in the dying minutes, but were held to a 2-2 scoreline by the time the final whistle was blown.

"It's a dream debut, I'm very happy," Huerta told TUDN after the match. "I want to dedicate this to all of my family and all the people that never stopped supporting [me]."

Draw in hand, Mexico will face Uzbekistan in a friendly next Tuesday, while Australia will have a few weeks to prepare for their next friendly against England on Oct. 13.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Mexico 0, Australia 1. Harry Souttar (Australia) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Martin Boyle with a cross   following a corner.
Mitchell Duke (Australia) is shown the yellow card.
First Half ends, Mexico 0, Australia 1.
Second Half begins Mexico 0, Australia 1.
Connor Metcalfe (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Penalty missed! Still  Mexico 0, Australia 1. Santiago Giménez (Mexico) hits the left post with a left footed shot.
Substitution, Mexico. Raúl Jiménez replaces Santiago Giménez.
Substitution, Mexico. César Huerta replaces Alexis Vega.
Goal!  Mexico 0, Australia 2. Martin Boyle (Australia) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the centre of the goal.
Substitution, Australia. Nick D'Agostino replaces Mitchell Duke.
Substitution, Australia. Denis Genreau replaces Connor Metcalfe.
Goal!  Mexico 1, Australia 2. Raúl Jiménez (Mexico) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Raúl Jiménez (Mexico) is shown the yellow card for excessive celebration.
Substitution, Australia. Samuel Silvera replaces Martin Boyle.
Substitution, Mexico. Jordi Cortizo replaces Orbelín Pineda.
Substitution, Mexico. Érick Sánchez replaces Héctor Herrera.
Substitution, Mexico. Roberto Alvarado replaces Uriel Antuna.
Substitution, Mexico. Kevin Álvarez replaces Julián Araujo.
Goal!  Mexico 2, Australia 2. César Huerta (Mexico) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Johan Vásquez.
Substitution, Australia. Aiden O'Neill replaces Jackson Irvine because of an injury.
Substitution, Australia. Milos Degenek replaces Keanu Baccus.
Second Half ends, Mexico 2, Australia 2.
